[[_TOC_]]

##List of differences between Prime PatConfig vs EVG PatternModify

|  |PatConfig (PRIME)  |PatternModify (EVG)  |
|--|--|--|
|Ratio  |Address offset will adjust according to Ratio.  |Address offset do not change.  |

##Prerequisites

<p style="font-size: 25px;"><span style="color:Red;">From Prime 6.0 FuseConfig *.fusedef.json are separated into *.fuseDef.json and *.fuseDataLabel.json</span></p> 

<p style="font-size: 25px;"><span style="color:Red;">From Prime 5.0 FuseConfig doesn't support multi fuse group in the same ConfigurationElement</span></p> 

```json
{
      "Name": "MultiElements",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "YIELDMON"  <--- This changed from array to string
        }
}
```


<p style="font-size: 25px;"><span style="color:yellow;">This Service is using Aleph initialization. Please refer to Aleph documentation</span></p> 

[<span style="color:lightblue;font-size:1.5em">Link to Aleph Documentation](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PrimeWiki/_wiki/wikis/PrimeWiki.wiki/28879/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime)



##New Fuse DEF file format

In order to use fuses in prime, we need to load the fuse definition file. In prime, we introduce a new format to the old fusedef file. The file will be in JSON format and will be arranged by fuse groups.
This file is different from the current LIPORT file.
The file will be auto-generated by flame.
Old fuse def file can be converted by the following script you will need to have the LIPORT auto-generated fusedef file and to create manually another text file that will have a separate line for each register in the fusedef file and will contain  the fuse register name, Label and offset in the following format (**this is not the pattern_setup.txt** file but you can get the relevant data from this file) :

config:  <Register Name> : <Label Name> : <Offset from label>:
Example:
       
        config:      CPU0:   FUSE_START_CPU0:    2:
        config:      PCH0:   FUSE_START_PCH0:    0:

Download the parser, unzip, open the console window and run the script,

Usage[1]: FuseDefParser.exe <Fusedef file> <label and offset file> <moduleName>
Usage[2]: FuseDefParser.exe <Fusedef file> <moduleName>
Usage[3]: FuseDefParser.exe <label and offset file> <moduleName>

Output filename will be : <moduleName>.fuseDef.json or <moduleName>.fuseDataLabel.json

[<span style="color:green;font-size:2em">**Click to Download FuseDefParser**</span>](./.attachments/FuseDefParser-0621e934-8d97-4d72-ad38-a5cdbd3616ea.zip)

```shell


MIT ATE Test Methods
FuseDefParser v2.0

Usage[1]: FuseDefParser.exe fuseDef.txt moduleName
Usage[2]: FuseDefParser.exe labelConfig.txt moduleName
Usage[3]: FuseDefParser.exe fuseDef.txt labelConfig.txt moduleName

Optional arguments:
        [-h],[--help],[?/] = Displays the parser help.

Positional arguments:
        [fuseDef.txt] = Fuse definition file used in FuseConfig Template.

        Example:

        ######################################
        #Operation:  Register: Type:   Length
        ######################################
        register:    CPO0:   binary: 50
        register:    PCH0:   binary: 50

        ##################################################################################
        #Operation:  Register:  Address:    Type:   Fuse Name:                   Fuse Group
        ##################################################################################
        fusedef:     CPO0:    1-0:          senr:   MANUFACT_HVM_SAMPLE:         MANUFACT_HVM_SAMPLE
        fusedef:     CPO0:    3-2,4:        senr:   MANUFACT_SORT_SCAN_RECLAIM:  MANUFACT_SORT_SCAN_RECLAIM
        fusedef:     PCH0:    10-5,15-11:   senr:   MANUFACT_SORT_CSE_FAIL:      MANUFACT_SORT_CSE_FAIL
        fusedef:     PCH0:    16:           senr:   MANUFACT_RESVD_1:            MANUFACT_RESVD_1

        #########################################
        #pin:  Pin Type:      Register:  Pin Name
        #########################################
        pin:   pin_to_modify: CPU0:      TDI
        pin:   pin_to_read:   CPU0:      TDO
        pin:   pin_to_modify: PCH0:      PCH_JTAG_TDI
        pin:   pin_to_read:   PCH0:      PCH_JTAG_TDO

        [labelConfig.txt] = Pattern Setup file used it need to be generated by the user using the following syntax:

        Example:

        #########################################################################################
        # Operation: Register: Label:            Offset:
        #########################################################################################
        config:      CPU0:   FUSE_START_CPU0:    2:
        config:      PCH0:   FUSE_START_PCH0:    0:

        [moduleName] = Module name will be used as output file name.

        Example:
        moduleName ="core" -> output filename = core.fusedef.json
        moduleName ="core" -> output filename = core.fuseDataLabel.json


```

All fuse def files need to end with ".fusedef.json". These files will be consumed by Aleph, see [ALEPH Files](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PRIME/_wiki/wikis/PRIME.wiki/181/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime?anchor=aleph_files)  for more setup information.

- Only MSB to LSB FuseDef file supported.
- Split fuse support with only one fuse inside a fuse group.

### Fuse DataLabel file format example
All files should end with .fuseDataLabel.json
```json
{
  "Registers": [
    {
      "RegisterName": ["CPU0"],
      "LabelName": "StartDr__FUSEIP_DFX_TAP__JTAG_STREAMING_WRITE__40962_1__RISINGEDGEDETECT",
      "LabelOffset": 2
    },
    {
      "RegisterName": ["CNP_LP"],
      "LabelName": "HVM_FUSE_START",
      "LabelOffset": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

## Fuses Configuration file format

All fuse config files need to end with ".fuseconfig.json". These files will be consumed by Aleph, see [ALEPH Files](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PRIME/_wiki/wikis/PRIME.wiki/181/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime?anchor=aleph_files)  for more setup information.

### **Legacy Mode**
By default, the fuse configuration file's format is the legacy one.
This legacy format includes the ratio and the pattern's regular expresion, and follows the following format:

```json

{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "Scenario_1",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P.*"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "SOCFuseGen_reserved_VirtualFusePointer_row_0_bit_16",
          "Data": "10101010111100001000000000000011"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "Name": "Scenario_2",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "YIELDMON",
          "Plists" : ["Plist1","Plist2"]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "Name": "MultiElements",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "YIELDMON"
        },
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "SOCFuseGen_reserved_VirtualFusePointer_row_0_bit_16"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
The configurations field holds a list of metadata and default data for the Fuse Config. The fields of the JSON are:

- **Name** 
Indicates the identifier of the Configuration, this value shall be used to refer to the configuration, set data, and apply to the pattern itself

- **ConfigurationElement**
Contain a list of elements to apply fuse config on. Each element contain the following

  - **Ratio** 
Optional Field that is a Data Modifier (default value of 1), if the ratio is applied, each Bit symbol will be multiplied by this ratio, also the address offset will be calculated automatically, for example:
      - fuse area start address (Fuse label + (offset that defined in the fuse def file* Ratio)): 100
      - FuseGroup : 10-15 (size 6 )
      - Ratio: 4
        
        fuse area start address = Label + (offset * 4) = 92 + (2*4) = 100
       Absolute start address : 100 + (10 * 4) = 140  
       Absolute end address : absolute start address + ( (ratio * fuse size) - 1 ) = 140 + ( (4 * 6) - 1) = 163


For example, if the ratio equal 2 and data is 1010, the service will refer to data as the user set *11001100*
  - **PatternsRegEx**
 List of regular expressions to apply the fuse config on.
 Note this regex should match patterns in order to be used (If the regex does not match patterns but is never used it should be ok, this is a request to enable engineering work).
  - **Register**
    Register name to apply data modification.
  - **FuseGroup**
     Group nape to apply the data modification.
  - **Data**
Optional field to let pre-define a value for the configuration, this value is used if the user doesn't override by setting a value using SetData or FillData API's, also when the user calls ResetToDefault API this value overrides any previous modification by means of SetData or FillData API.
  - **Value Format**
    - **SetData** : The value that should apply to the pin, size must match the expected size.
        Example "Data" : "10101"
    - **Fill Data** : The Symbol that should be applied to the pin following by the "+" sign
        Example : "Data" : "1+"
  - **Value apply order**
When applying data to a configuration the size of the data will be the total number of bits for all "ConfigurationElement". If the Configuration contains more than one "ConfigurationElement" the data will be applied by the order of the elements. for example :
In the fuse config above we have configuration "MultiElements" 
```json
{
      "Name": "MultiElements",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "YIELDMON"
        },
    {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegEx": ["P1","P2"],
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroup": "SOCFuseGen_reserved_VirtualFusePointer_row_0_bit_16"
        }
```
this configuration contains 2 elements
let assume the size first element (with fuse group "YIELDMON") is 10 bits and the second element contains 16 bits. The total number of bits we need to supply is 26 bits. when we will set the date the first 10 bits will go to the first element  (with fuse group "YIELDMON") and the remaining 16 bits will go to the second element



If a group contain more than one fuse for example let assume that we have the following definition in the FuseDefFile

```json
{
   "Name": "GROUP_A",
   "Fuses": [
     {
        "Name": "FUSE1",
        "StartAddress": [5],
        "EndAddress": [0]
     },
     {
        "Name": "FUSE2",
        "StartAddress": [9],
        "EndAddress": [6]
     }
   ]
},
```
The group size is 10, when we supply the following data the first bit is the MSB and the last bit is the LSB.
The MSB bit will be set to the address with the highest value  
![image.png](./.attachments/image-1d039392-54e0-404c-a542-10e74de9b5c9.png)



   - **Plists**
optional field, in case the user configure the Plist element the regular expression will be matched against all patterns that we have in the plist instead of all loaded patterns for example :
If the following regular expression Pattern.* will return :  
     -   Pattern1
     -   Pattern2
     -   Pattern3

       and we have the following Plist :
       Plist1 
       {
            &nbsp;&nbsp; Pre1
            &nbsp;&nbsp; Pattent1
       }
adding element :  "Plists" : ["Plist1"] to the JSON will match the Regular expression on all patterns on Plist1 (RegEx Pattern.* will match only on Pattern1) and the data will be applied only on Pattern1.

#### **Fuse Configuration element idetifier**

In order to access a specific fuse configuration element the name identifier is the name of the fuse group (see below API section for more details)

### **Flame Mode**

The original format for fuse configurations has the problem that includes the pattern regex and the ration. Flame does not have this information, so having that requirement in Prime gates the usage of Flame's out-of-the-box fuse configuration files, breaking the whole idea of having a POR tool generating these files.

Bacause Flame does not have that information, they cannot provide it. So, we have re-designed the fuse configuration file's format together with the Flame's team.

```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "Scenario_1",
      "ConfigurationElements": [
        {
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroups": [
            {
              "Name": "FUSE4",
              "Data": "0000"
            },
            {
              "Name": "FUSE5",
              "Data": "0000"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "Name": "Scenario_2",
      "ConfigurationElements": [
        {
          "Register": "CPU0",
          "FuseGroups": [
            {
              "Name": "FUSE4",
              "Data": "0000"
            },
            {
              "Name": "FUSE5",
              "Data": "0000"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Register": "CPU1",
          "FuseGroups": [
            {
              "Name": "FUSE2",
              "Data": "0000"
            },
            {
              "Name": "FUSE3",
              "Data": "0000"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

While the layout is a little bit different than the previous file, grouping the FuseGroups, the main difference is that this file no longer includes the PatRegex and the Ratio, as we commented before.

To use this format/layout, the user must set in the environment variable `FUSE_CONFIG_MODE="FLAME"`. If this environment variable doesn't exist, or is set to something different than `FLAME`, the legacy mode would be enabled as default.

This change has a big implication on the usage of the service and any other service or methodology using a fuse configuration: the pattern regex and the ratio are now provided by the user of the fuse configuration. The ratio is optional, and the PatConfigService takes a `ratio=1` as default all the time. But, there is no default for the pattern's regular expression, so they are now mandatory. This means that services such as VoltageService and FuseReadService and test method such as the _PatConfig_ and FuseReadMarginSweep have now an setting which is optional if the TP is on `FUSE_CONFIG_MODE="LEGACY"`, but mandatory if `FUSE_CONFIG_MODE="FLAME"`.

The flame mode also removes the duality of having two places to set the pattern's regular expression. In the legacy mode, the users do not have clarity of what is the final criteria for the pattern filtering. Is using the one in the fuse configuration? Or the we provided? Or is applying both? We see beneficial having one source of the pattern's regular expression as it's more clear to the user how the patterns will be filtered.

## Patterns Modify file format 

All pattern modify files need to end with ".patmod.json". These files are consumed by Aleph, see [ALEPH Files](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PRIME/_wiki/wikis/PRIME.wiki/181/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime?anchor=aleph_files)  for more information on setup.

**JSON FILE EXAMPLE**
This File shows a configuration file for PinData, Instruction, and a mix of Instruction, Pindata, and PVC(CTV,MTV, and LRPT). In the example, we have multiple configurations in the form of a list with various valid scenarios, for simplification offsets had been set to 0, but could have also been set to any numeric value or label as long as the sum with the start and end addresses matches with the provided data size.

```json
{
    "Configurations": [
        {
            "Name": "RatioChange",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {               
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "RatioChangePCH",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "RatioLabel",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "RatioLabel",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*",
                        "P.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        },
{
            "Name": "MultiPin",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {               
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": ["TDI","TMS"],
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "InstructionPatModify1",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "INSTRUCTION",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "101",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ],
                    "Plists" : ["Plist1","Plist2"]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "InstructionPatModify2",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "Instruction",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "MyLabel",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "MyLabel",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "RPT 100"
                }
            ]
        },
{
            "Name": "MultiDomain",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "Instruction",
                    "Domain": ["LEG","DDR"],
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": ["pat1.*"]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "MixConfig",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Name": "WaitStage",
                    "Type": "Instruction",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "MyLabel",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "MyLabel",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "RPT 100"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Name" : "ApplyCTV",
                    "Type": "CTV",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "MTV",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "LRPT",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1|10|11|12"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```

**Compressed json file example**
```json
{
    "Configurations": [
        {
            "Name": "RatioChange",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": ["100","MyLabel"],
                    "StartAddressOffset": [0,0],
                    "EndAddress": ["107","MyLabel"],
                    "EndAddressOffset": [0,7],
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "InstructionPatModify1",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "INSTRUCTION",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": ["100","200"],
                    "StartAddressOffset": [0,0],
                    "EndAddress": ["100","207"],
                    "EndAddressOffset": [7,0],
                    "patternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

```
There is support for pattern regexes across multiple configurations, note that the following 3 RatioChange configurations are equivalent:

```json

    "Configurations": [

                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "RatioChange2",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "P.*"
                    ],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "RatioChange3",
                        "PatternsRegEx": [
                           "P.*"
                    ],
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [],
                    "Data": "1+"
                },
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [],
                    "Data": "1+"
                }
            ]
        },
       ]
}
```
The configurations field holds a list of metadata and default data for Pattern Modify. The fields of the JSON are:

- **Name**
 Configuration name, this is the name that the user will need in order to get the configuration, set data, and apply it to the pattern itself
- **ConfigurationElement**
Contain a list of elements to apply pattern modify on. Each element contain the following
  - **Type**
  Supported types for ConfigurationElement are: 
    - PINDATA
    - INSTRUCTION
    - CTV
    - MTV
    - LRPT

**Patterns Modify Field Description**

##Common fields
There are two formats for the start and end address and offsets, a regular one where the user defines the actual address or a compressed format where the user defines a list of start and end address and offsets. In the case of user-defined lists, the sizes of all lists must be the same. In the compressed mode, the service will take the data per index.

   - **Name**
**Optional** field, allow the user to access a specific configuration element and set data, fill data, reset to default and more, see API section for more details.  
   - **StartAddress**
 List of start addresses or a specific start address to apply to modification. The value can be an absolute address, Label, or Label Regular expression
    - **StartAddressOffset**
  List of Offsets or an offset from the start address 
     - **EndAddress**
  List of end addresses or a specific end address to apply to modification. The value can be an absolute address, Label, Label Regular expression, or special char "^" ( see below for an explanation on address calculation)
  - **EndAddressOffset**
  List of Offsets or an offset from the end address
  - **PatternsRegEx**
  List of regular expressions that will be used to collect patterns based on pattern names into a set and then apply the specified modification.
  Note this regex should match patterns in order to be used (If the regex does not match patterns but is never used it should be ok, this is a request to enable engineering work).
   - **Plists**
optional field, in case the user configure the Plist element the regular expression will be matched against all patterns that we have in the plist instead of all loaded patterns for example :
If the following regular expression Pattern.* will return :  
     -   Pattern1
     -   Pattern2
     -   Pattern3

       and we have the following Plist :
       Plist1 
       {
            &nbsp;&nbsp; Pre1
            &nbsp;&nbsp; Pattent1
       }
adding element :  "Plists" : ["Plist1"] to the JSON will match the Regular expression on all patterns on Plist1 (RegEx Pattern.* will match only on Pattern1) and the data will be applied only on Pattern1.

  - **ValidationMode**
This is an optional token, the value for this token is always ALLOW_LABEL_NO_MATCHING.
    -  **without the token or empty token**: during init for each pattern that we need to modify the service will check if the label exists in the pattern. If it doesn't exist the init will fail. example
"ValidationMode" : ""
    -  **Token value ALLOW_LABEL_NO_MATCHING**: In this case, if the label didn't found in the pattern this pattern will be skipped and the modifications will not be applied to this pattern. Only if the service will not found this label in all patterns (so we don't have anything to modify) the init will fail.
"ValidationMode" : "ALLOW_LABEL_NO_MATCHING"

**Example for start and end Address**

-  _Regular addresses_
"StartAddress": "100",
"StartAddressOffset": 0,
"EndAddress": "104",
"EndAddressOffset": 3,

In this case, the absolute start addresses will be 100+0 = 100, the absolute end address will 104+3 = 107, the data will be applied from address 100 to 107 total of 8 bits

- _Compressed addresses_
"StartAddress": ["100","MyLabel"],
"StartAddressOffset": [0,0],
"EndAddress": ["107","MyLabel"],
"EndAddressOffset": [0,7],

In this case, the data will be applied to :
1. For Index 0 :  Absolute start address 100+0 = 100, absolute end address 107+0=107. The data will be applied from address 100 to 107 total of 8 bits
2. For Index 1 :  For calculation let assume that MyLabel address is 1000. Absolute start address MyLabel+0=1000+0=1000, absolute end address MyLabel+7=1000+7=1007. The data will be applied from address 1000 to 1007 total of 8 bits.

The size of each calculation per index(Index 0 and 1) is the same.

- **SET PINDATA**
  - **IsChannelLink (Optional default false)**
A Boolean that represents if the current configuration is for a linked channel. A linked channel term is referred to as the utilization of multiple contiguous channel memory as a single unit of memory that belongs to a specified channel. This is used to support long patterns.
  - **Pin**
A pin name or a list of pins to apply the modify on
  - **Ratio**
The ratio to apply for the data (optional field default is 1), if the ratio is applied, each Bit symbol will be multiplied by this ratio, for example, if ratio equal 2 and datש is 1010, the service will refer to data as the user set *11001100*
  - **Data**
Optional field, let the user to pre-define value for the configuration, this value will be used if the user didn't set any value (using SetData or FillData API's) or if the user uses ResetToDefault API.
      - **Set Pin Data**
 The value that should apply to the pin, the size must match the expected size. skip character is allowed ("_"), each char will skip one vector
 Examples:
 "Data" : "10101"
 "Data" : "101_1"
**Note**: When using in channel link vectors to partially override a vector, the actual value will read modify and then overwrite with the specified, as an example with a channel link with 2 channels, to set every odd bit and keep the pair locations just set '__' character to keep the value as is already in the vector.
  Example:
  Having a pattern with 1111 in the location to set the data and just rewrite it to 1010 it is just needed:
"Data" : "_0_0"
      - **Fill** providing Pin Data 
The Symbol that should be applied to the pin following by the "+" sign
Example : "Data" : "1+"

- **SET INSTRUCTION DATA**
  - **Domain**
  A Domain or a list of domains that refers to the HDMT pattern Domains to apply the modification to.
  An example for Domain could be DDR, LEG. 
  - **Data**
Optional field to let the user to pre-define value for the configuration, this value will be used if the user didn't set any value (using SetData or FillData API's) or if the user uses ResetToDefault API.
    - **Set**
The value that should apply to the domain, the number of instructions must match the expected size. Instruction needs to be separated by a pipe delimiter ("|").
Skipping a vector is provided by using underscore ("\_") in the instruction and count as a vector, each skip char should be separated by a delimiter and count as a single vector.
The skip character can also be used for the partial skip of updating an instruction, useful to keep the opcode(instruction name) with different arguments, or just replacing a single argument of the instruction.
Example: For the following vectors

|Vector  | data |
|--|--|
| 1 | RPT 20 |
| 2 | DEC R10  |
| 3 | MOV 20,R1 |
| 4 | MOV 25,R2 |

When applying the following data **"RPT 10| _ |MOV 30,_|MOV _,R6"** to the configuration that modifies 4 vectors
- first vector instruction  RPT 10 will be applied.
- Second vector will be skipped.
- third vector needs to apply MOV 30,_ .This means that we will use the same second argument (register R1) that we have at the original instruction and replaced only the first argument with a value 30.
- fourth vector need to apply MOV _,R6 .This means that we will use the first argument value (25) and replace only the second argument (Register R2) with R6.

After the modify we will have :

|Vector  | data |
|--|--|
| 1 | RPT 10 |
| 2 | DEC R10  |
| 3 | MOV 30,R1 |
| 4 | MOV 25,R6 |

    - **Fill**
 Not supported in instruction nor for PVC, an exception is thrown in case of a call.
- **PVC (CTV,MTV,LRPT)**
The value that should apply to the domain, the number of PVCs must match the expected size. LRPT case works as instruction that needs to be separated by a pipe delimiter ("|"), CTV and MTV work as pindata with no special separation needed. Skipping a vector is provided by using underscore ("_") in any PVC, each skip char should be separated by a delimiter and count as a single vector.
    - **Set Data**
This mechanism supports any of the PVCs (CTV, MTV, LRPT).
Example **LRPT**
**"10|\_|\_|20"**
Example **CTV**
**"1001"**
Example **MTV**
**"1001"**
 The instruction has 4 vectors, the second and third vector will be skipped
    - **Fill**
 Not supported for instruction, neither for LRPT, an exception is thrown in case of a call. Supported by Pindata, CTV, and MTV.
Example **CTV**
**"1+"**
Example **MTV**
**"1+"**

###Configuration with multiple types of Configuration elements
Each Configuration can have multiple ConfigurationElement from different types.
Setting/Fill data only support if all ConfigurationElements are from the same type.
If the types are different we **must have default data for all ConfigurationElement**.
Trying to set/fill data with mixed types will throw an exception.

####Setting data For multiple pindata
If we have multiple ConfigurationElement that are all from type PINDATA for example :
```json
{
            "Name": "MultiplePinData",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "Pin": "TDO",
                    "StartAddress": "MYLABEL",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "MYLABEL",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 7,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 3,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        ".*pre.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }

```
It can be inferred that:
- first ConfigurationElement size is 8 and we want to apply data LHLHHHHH
- second ConfigurationElement size is 4 and we want to apply 1010

When data is set with LHLHHHHH1010, the service will automatically take the correct data according to the size of each ConfigurationElement

####Setting data For multiple Instruction
If we have multiple ConfigurationElement that are all from type PINDATA for example :
```json
              {
            "Name": "MultipleInstructions",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "Instruction",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "MYLABEL",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "MYLABEL",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 1,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "Type": "Instruction",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "1000",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "1001",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 7,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "pat1.*",
                        "pat2.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }

```
It can be inferred that 
- first ConfigurationElement size is 2 and we want to apply data RPT 50|RET
- second ConfigurationElement size is 4 and we want to apply RPT 10

It's going to set data with RPT 50|RET|RPT 10, the service will automatically take the correct data according to the size of each ConfigurationElement

### Address Calculation
The **absolute start address** in all cases is equal to "StartAddress" + StartAddressOffset
The **absolute end address** in all cases is equal to "EndAddress " + EndAddressOffset 

StartAddress can be a number, a Label, or Label regular expression
EndAddress can be a number, a Label, or a special char "^" in case that the start address will be a regular expression.
StartAddressOffset and EndAddressOffset are always a number

**Example of Absolute address calculation for start and end address as numbers with offset:**

```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 5,
          "EndAddress": "107",
          "EndAddressOffset": 10,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

absolute start address = StartAddress(100) + StartAddressOffset(5) = 105
absolute endaddress = EndAddress (107) + EndAddressOffset (10) = 117

**Example of Absolute address calculation for labels with offset:**

```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "StartLabel",
          "StartAddressOffset": 5,
          "EndAddress": "EndLabel",
          "EndAddressOffset": 10,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

 P1
  - StartLabel : Address 100
  - EndLabel : Address 103

absolute start address = StartLabel (100) + StartAddressOffset(5) = 105
absolute endaddress = EndLabel (103) + EndAddressOffset (10) = 113

In case when both StartAddress and EndAddress are labels and the PatternsRegEx match more than one pattern, the size between the start and end address from all patterns should be the same for example for the following configuration :
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "StartLabel",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "EndLabel",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P.*"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
If the regular expression match to P1 & P2
- P1
  - StartLabel : Address 100
  - EndLabel : Address 103
- P2
   - StartLabel : Address 120
   - EndLabel : Address 125

In the example, the size of the modification for P1 is 4, and P2 is 6.  This configuration will fail during init.

### Reverse Data
On PatModify  <span style="color: #ff0000;">for PINDATA only</span> we can configure our json to reverse the data for us. This will be done when the absolute start address is greater then absolute end address.

An example of a start and end address as a number:


```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "103",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "100",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
Setting the value "1010" will result as 
|Address|data|
|-------|----|
|100    | 0  |
|101    | 1  |
|102    | 0  |
|103    | 1  |

```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDO",
          "StartAddress": "StartLabel",
          "StartAddressOffset": 3,
          "EndAddress": "StartLabel",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
- P1
  - StartLabel : Address 100

absolute start address = StartLabel (100) + StartAddressOffset(3) = 103
absolute end address   = StartLabel (100) + StartAddressOffset(0) = 100

Setting the value "LHXZ" will result as 
|Label|Address|data|
|------------|-------|----|
|StartLabel  |100    | Z  |
|            |101    | X  |
|            |102    | H  |
|            |103    | L  |


###Restricted Fuse Area

Pattern Modify isn't allowed in the fuse config area, if any configuration will be defined on fuse modify pin at addresses that belong to fuse will fail during the Init. 
The restricted area is defined by 
Start Fuse Area Address = <Fuse Label> + offset
End Fuse Area Address = <Start Fuse Area Address> + <Fuse Area Size> - 1
For example, if in the fusedef file we have the following data :
- Label = MyLabel = 98
- Offset = 2
- size = 50
- Pin = TDO

Start Fuse Area Address = 98 + 2 = 100
End Fuse Area Address = 100 + 50 - 1 = 149
Any Pattern Modify that will be applied from on TDO pin between address 100 to 149 will fail.

###Ratio Usage
- Absolute Address
PatConfig to multiply raw data with ratio only. Raw_Data * Ratio = Final_Data. Absolute address of StartAddress + StartAddressOffset, and EndAddress + EndAddressOffset will not be adjusted. 
For example,
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "107",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ],
          "Ratio": 2,
          "Data": "1010"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
End Result:
Data to modify = 11001100
Start Address = (StartAddress + StartAddressOffset) = 100 + 0 = 100
End Address = (EndAddress + EndAddressOffset) = 107 + 0 = 107

- Label
PatConfig to multiply raw data with ratio, and adjust offset accordingly with ratio.
For example,
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDI",
          "StartAddress": "StartLabel",
          "StartAddressOffset": 1,
          "EndAddress": "StartLabel",
          "EndAddressOffset": 4,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1"
          ],
          "Ratio": 2,
          "Data": "1010"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
Assuming StartLabel is at address 100.

End Result:
Data to modify = 11001100
Start Address: (StartAddress + (StartAddressOffset * Ratio)) = 100 + (1*2) = 102
End Address: (Start Address) + (Data_to_modify_size - 1) = 102 + (8-1) = 109

- Absolute Address + Label - **Not supported**.


###Collision Check For Modify Locations
There is an environment variable to enable/disable the collision check for memory locations. It is recommended not to enable the collision check during production as it will significantly increase the init test time.
The purpose of collision check on the modify locations is to make sure current handle will not write to the same modify location as any other element handles in the configuration.
The details of the environment variable is as below:
Environement Variable name: "ENABLE_MODIFY_LOCATIONS_COLLISION_CHECK",
Description: "Enable the Collision check on the modify locations.",
Type: Choice (either "TRUE" or "FALSE"),
IsRequired = false,
DefaultBehaviorWhenNotExist = "FALSE" (PatConfigService will skip collision check on modify locations.))

##IDUT Support
###Define Pin/Domain in Configuration file
In order to access a Pin or a Domain that are inside and IP we must add as a prefix the IP name, for example :
- if we want to access to Pin TDI that is inside IP CPU the value should be CPU::TDO
- if we want to access Domain LEG that is inside IP PCH we must write PCH::LEG
- if we want to access pin/domain in PKG we don't need to have any prefix for example TDO or DDR

###Configuration building
During Aleph Init when parsing the Configuration, the regular expression may match to patterns that don't have any domain that matches the Pin/Domain IP. in this case it will detect this and will skip this pattern. If we want to have a configuration that has more than one pin/Domain the code will detect for each pin if the pattern is in an IP and will skip if it doesn't belong.

**For Example :** 

![image.png](./.attachments/image-20b58db4-f81f-4eab-b1b3-62668d52fb67.png) 
In the above picture we have PKG level and two IPs CPU and PCH
In PKG :
- Pattern : Pattern_PKG
- Pins TDI & TDO

In CPU :
- Pattern : Pattern_CPU
- Pins TDI & CPU_PIN

In PCH:
- Pattern : Pattern_PCH
- Pins JTAG_TDI & PCH_PIN

For the following configuration :

```json
{
    "Configurations": [
        {
            "Name": "MultiPin",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": [
                        "TDI",
                        "CPU::TDI"
                    ],
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "Pattern_.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```
As mentioned before you can see that in the configuration we have TDI and CPU::TDI,
- TDI -belongs to PKG level only and will not match the CPU TDI Pin
- CPU::TDI - belong to CPU IP only.

The Regular expression Pattern_.* will match the following patterns:
- Pattern_PKG
- Pattern_CPU
- Pattern_PCH

when going over the pin and the patterns we will get 

![image.png](./.attachments/image-0782755d-101d-4261-a9ca-7d4d5037bc38.png)

Since in the configuration we don't have any pin/domain that belongs to PCH Ip wi will skip Pattern_PCH.
The configuration will be :

```
Pattern_PKG
- IP : PKG
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 103 
- Pin : TDI
Pattern_CPU
- IP : CPU
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 103 
- Pin : CPU::TDI
```

###Handle building from instance **inside an IP**
When our instance is inside an IP when getting the handle
- User code
- Using PatConfigTestMethod
- Using PatConfig Common params

The PatModify handle will contain only patterns that are inside the IP, in the above example if we are inside IP CPU the handle will contain only CPU patterns

```
Pattern_CPU
- IP : CPU
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 103 
- Pin : CPU::TDI
```
If we will call from an IP that the configuration doesn't have any data getting the handle will fail, in the above example if we will try to get from PCH IP we will fail.

###Handle building from instance **at PKG Level**
From the PKG level, we can apply Modification to PKG patterns and also to patterns that belong to any IP.
We can define if we want to apply the modification to PKG only or to all the IPs that we have in the configuration.
**_The default behaviour is to apply to all IPs._**
In the above example in case we will define to Apply to PKG only the following will be applied :
```
Pattern_PKG
- IP : PKG
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 103 
- Pin : TDI

``` 
In the above example if we will define to apply to all IPs we will apply the PKG and CPU (since this is what the configuration define).

```json
{
    "Configurations": [
        {
            "Name": "MultiDomain",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "INSTRUCTION",
                    "Domain": [
                        "LEG",
                        "CPU::LEG",
                        "PCH::DDR"
                    ],
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "100",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "Pattern_.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```
For the above configuration, we will have the following configuration modification
```
Pattern_PKG
- IP : PKG
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 100 
- Domain: LEG
Pattern_CPU
- IP : CPU
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 100
- Domain: CPU::LEG
Pattern_PCH
- IP : PCH
- Start Address : 100
- End Address  : 100
- Domain: PCH::DDR
```

And applying to all IPs will apply to all modifications.
For more information about how to configure apply yo all IP's or PKG see Get Handle APIs below or PatConfigTestMethod wiki.

##APIs description
###Get Handle APIs
In order to apply data to configuration, the user must get a handle for the required configuration.
These APIs will return an object that allows setting the data to the configuration.

- **Advance options**
In order to support the advance options, a new class AdvanceGetHandleOptions will need to configure and be used in the relevant APIs.

   
```
public class AdvanceGetHandleOptions
{
    bool InPKGApplyToPKGOnly;
}
```
 - InPKGApplyToPKGOnly - allow the user to configure that if we are in PKG we will apply only the modification that relevant to PKG only or to apply all the modifications in the configuration.
    - Relevant to PKG only, ignored if the instance is inside an IP
    - If True - Apply to PKG only
    - If False - Apply to all IPs only
    - Default is to apply to ALL IPs (false)

- **Get Handle**
- _**GetPatConfigHandle ( configurationName )**_
This is the basic get handle, the user will give a configuration name and will get handle to this configuration
- _**GetPatConfigHandle ( configurationName, AdvanceGetHandleOptions )**_
Same as above,  this is the basic get handle, the user will give a configuration name and will get handle to this configuration, the user can also configure advanced options.


- _**GetPatConfigHandle ( configurationName, regEx )**_
In this API  the user can give an additional regular expression in order to reduce the number of patterns for his own usage, for example:

For configuration: 
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "MyConfig",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDO",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pre.*"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
} 
```
Loaded patterns = Pre_BF_1, Pre_BF_2,Pre_CACHE_1
Using the GetHandle with only the configuration name will  apply the modification on all loaded patterns (Pre_BF_1, Pre_BF_2,Pre_CACHE_1)
using the API with regEx GetHandle("MyConfig",".\*BF.*") will check all patterns that are included in the configuration with the additional regular expression, and the modifications will be applied only on the patterns that match. in our example only Pre_BF_1, Pre_BF_2 has a match.
- _**GetPatConfigHandle ( configurationName, regEx , AdvanceGetHandleOptions )**_
Same as above, in this API  the user can give an additional regular expression in order to reduce the number of patterns for his own usage, and also configure advanced options.


_**Get Handle with Plist**_
When getting a handle the user can ask for a handle that will apply patConfig only on patterns that exist in his plist and not all patterns that exist in a configuration, for example:

Loaded patterns =  Pre_BF_1, Pre_BF_2, Pre_CACHE_1
Using Configuration MyConfig from above: PatternsRegEx = Pre.*  (all patterns that start with Pre)
Plist:
```json
    GlobalPList  MyPlist{
       Pat Pre_CACHE_1;
    }
```
In this case, if we get a handle for "MyConfig" configuration with Plist "MyPlist", the handle will have only Pre_CACHE_1and the changes will be applied on P2.

- _**GetPatConfigHandleWithPlist( configurationName, plist)**_
This is the basic get handle, the user will give a configuration name, and plist name and will get handle to this configuration

- _**GetPatConfigHandleWithPlist( configurationName, plist, AdvanceGetHandleOptions)**_
Same as above, this is the basic get handle, the user will give a configuration name, and plist name and will get handle to this configuration and also configure advanced options.

- _**GetPatConfigHandleWithPlist( configurationName, plist, regEx)**_
This is the same as the above handle but an additional filter will be done with a regEx the handle will contain the intersection between patterns in the configuration reduced by the regEx and the patterns that are inside the plist
Loaded patterns =  Pre_BF_1, Pre_BF_2,Pre_CACHE_1
Using Configuration MyConfig from above: PatternsRegEx = Pre.*  (all patterns that start with Pre)
Plist:
```json
    GlobalPList  MyPlist{
       Pat Pre_BF_1;
       Pat Pre_BF_2;
    }
```

when using GetHandle("MyConfig", "MyPlist",".\*1.*")
we will get that the handle will contain only Pre_BF_1 since it is the only pattern that contains 1 in the name and inside MyPlist also.


- _**GetPatConfigHandleWithPlist( configurationName, plist, regEx , AdvanceGetHandleOptions)**_
This is the same as the above handle but we can also configure advanced options.

_GetStorableHandles()_
Gets list of all the configuration handles that are marked as storable.
**Currently not available for fuseConfig**

###Set Data APIs

_**SetData(data)**_
 This API belongs to the handle. This API let the user set data that will be applied
-  **PINDATA**
Set string with legal value 0,1,X,Z,L,H 
**Example:** "LHLHXX"
-  **CTV**
Set string with legal value 0 - disable CTV, 1 - Enable CTV
-  **MTV**
Set string with legal value 0 - disable MTV, 1 - Enable MTV
- **LRPT**
Set value for LRPT (value should be 1-32), the LRPT values are separated by pipe ("|") delimiter.
- **Instruction**
Set a string with Instructions syntax.
Each instruction composite from the Operation followed by space, and the operand for the instruction. The instructions are separated by pipe ("|") delimiter.
**Example:** "MOV 10,R1|RPT 100"

Here is the supported instruction :

|Operations              |Operands                  |Description                           |
|------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------|
|RPT                     |<int>                     |Repeat 32bit int                      |
|MUL                     |<register,<src1>,<src2>   |register = src1 * src2                |
|RET                     |                          |Normal Termination                    |
|MOV                     |<int>,<register>          |Load value into register              |
|DEC                     |<register>                |Decrement by 1                        |
|JMP                     |<label>                   |Jump to label                         |
|JNZ                     |<label>                   |Jump to label if zero flag            |
|EXTTrigger              |LevelTC::<TriggerName>    |Apply levels test condition           |
|TOSTrigger              |<payload>                 |Callback with payload argument        |
|ClearSoftwareTrigger    |                          |Clear SW trigger prior to usage       |
|JNSTR                   |<label>                   |Jump not SW trigger, loop while       |
|                        |                          |TOSTrigger is handled by User Code    |
|CALL                    |<label>                   |Call label existing within subroutine |
|IGNOREFAILSON           |                          |Failures will not be reported         |
|IGNOREFAILSOFF          |                          |End of not reporting failures         |

_**SetData(data,elementIndex)**_
will set data to a specific element inside the handle by its index (first element index is 0) this will throw an exception if the index is out of range.

_**SetData(data,elementName)**_
will set data to a specific element inside the handle by its name (if applicable), this will throw an exception if the name doesn't exist.

_**FillData(symbol)**_
This API belongs to the handle. This API lets the user set a symbol for all configuration in order to fill the same symbol in the range that define in the configuration file
-  **PINDATA**
 This lets the user set a symbol, this symbol will fill all data vectors addresses in the handle.
Available symbols are  ZERO, ONE, L, H, X, Z 
- **Instruction**
**Fill Data not supported for Instruction**, an exception will the thrown in case of calling this API.
-  **CTV**
 This lets the user set a symbol, this symbol will fill all vector addresses in the handle.
Available symbols are 
    - ZERO - Disable CTV
    - ONE - Enable CTV
-  **MTV**
 This lets the user set a symbol, this symbol will fill all vector addresses in the handle.
Available symbols are 
    - ZERO - Disable MTV
    - ONE - Enable MTV
- **LRPT**
**Fill Data not supported for LRPT**, an exception will the thrown in case of calling this API.

_**FillData(symbol,elementIndex)**_
This API lets the user set a symbol for a specific configuration element in the handle by its index (first element index is 0) in order to fill the same symbol in the range that define in the configuration file, this will throw an exception if the index is out of range.
_**FillData(symbol,elementName)**_
This API lets the user set a symbol for a specific configuration element in the handle by its name (if applicable) in order to fill the same symbol in the range that define in the configuration file, this will throw an exception if the name doesn't exist.

### Reset API
_**ResetToDefault()**_
This API will Reset to default the handle data.
The value will be :
- Default value was set by the user in the configuration file :
 The current value for the configuration will be the default value, for example, if the user configured the "Data" : "0101" current handle value will be set to 0101 and will be used to apply to pattern
- No Default value :
Data will be cleared, if the user will try to apply to pattern the user will get an exception.

_**ResetToDefault(elementIndex)**_
This API will Reset to default data a specific configuration element in the handle by its index (first element index is 0), this will throw an exception if the index is out of range.
_**ResetToDefault(elementName)**_
This API will Reset to default data a specific configuration element in the handle by its name (if applicable), this will throw an exception if the name doesn't exist.


### Expected Size API's
_**GetNumberOfConfigurationElements()**_
This API returns the number of ConfigurationElements in the handle

_**GetExpectedDataSize()**_
Get Expected data size for current Handle for all ConfigurationElements inside the handle

_**GetExpectedDataSize(configurationElementIndex)**_
Get Expected data size for a specific ConfigurationElement inside the handle by its index (first element index is 0), this will throw an exception if the index is out of range.
_**GetExpectedDataSize(configurationElementName)**_
Get Expected data size for a specific ConfigurationElement inside the handle by its name (if applicable), this will throw an exception if the name doesn't exist.

### GetPatternsName API's
_**GetPatternsName()**_
Get a list of all pattern names that will be modified by this handle.


#### Skip Char
The service supports the skip char underscore ("_") when the user uses the SetData API. Each occurrence means a single vector skip. The total number of vectors to be changed should match the configuration.

##### **PINDATA Example**

```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "PindataPatModify",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "TDO",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 3,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pattern1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

For "PindataPatModify" configuration we expecting to modify 4 PINDATA vectors from start address 100 up to 103, User will need to set a total of 4 chars for example for the following data "L_HX" 

- Address 100: L will be set
- Address 101: This vector will be skipped
- Address 102: H will be set
- Address 103: X will be set
This can be used to skip pin data vectors, or if we have in the middle of our pin data vectors instruction vector.

##### **Instruction Example**
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "InstructionPatModify",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "INSTRUCTION",
          "Domain": "LEG",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pattern1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
For the "InstructionPatModify" configuration we are expecting to modify 4 INSTRUCTION vectors from start address 100 up to 103, User will need to set a total of 4 instructions, for example for the following data "MOV 10,R1|\_|\_|RPT 50" 

- Address 100: MOV 10,R1 will be set
- Address 101: This vector will be skipped
- Address 102: This vector will be skipped
- Address 103: RPT 50 will be set
This can be used to skip instruction vectors, or if we have in the middle of our instruction vector pin data vectors.

##### **CTV Example**
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "CtvPatModify",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "CTV",
          "Domain": "LEG",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pattern1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
For the "CtvPatModify" configuration we are expecting to modify 4 CTV vectors from start address 100 up to 103, User will need to set a total of 4 Ctvs, for example for the following data "1__1" (notice there are two underscores in the middle)

- Address 100: 1 will be set
- Address 101: This vector will be skipped
- Address 102: This vector will be skipped
- Address 103: 1 will be set
This can be used to skip instruction vectors or pindata vectors that are not required to be modified.

**Notes:**

1. If the vector type is an Instruction, CTV can't be applied to that vector, Ctv can only be applied to PinData Vectors.
2. Notice there is Domain instead of PinName for the data structure

##### **MTV Example**
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "MtvPatModify",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "MTV",
          "Domain": "LEG",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pattern1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
For "MtvPatModify" configuration we are expecting to modify 4 MTV vectors from start address 100 up to 103, User will need to set a total of 4 MTVs, for example for the following data "1__0" (notice 2 underscores in the middle)

- Address 100: 1 will be set
- Address 101: This vector will be skipped
- Address 102: This vector will be skipped
- Address 103: 0 will be set
This can be used to skip instruction vectors or pindata vectors that are not required to be modified. 

**Notes:**

1. If the vector type is Instruction, Mtv cannot be applied to that vector, Mtv can only be applied to PinData Vectors.
2. Notice there is Domain instead of PinName for the data structure


##### **LRPT Example**
```json
{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "MtvPatModify",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "LRPT",
          "Domain": "LEG",
          "StartAddress": "100",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "103",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "Pattern1"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
For the "LrptPatModify" configuration we are expecting to modify 4 LRPT vectors from start address 100 up to 103, User will need to set a total of 4 LRPTs, for example for the following data "10|\_|_|20" (notice 2 underscores in the middle)

- Address 100: 10 will be set
- Address 101: This vector will be skipped
- Address 102: This vector will be skipped
- Address 103: 10 will be set

This can be used to skip instruction vectors or pindata vectors that are not required to be modified. 

**Notes:**
1. If the vector type is Instruction, LRPT cannot be applied to that vector, LRPT can only be applied to PinData Vectors.
2. Notice there is Domain instead of PinName for the data structure


### Apply APIs
_**This API's should be called only after the user set data or the user-defined default data in the configuration file or else this call will fail and throw exception**_

_Apply(one-handle)_
This API get a handle and apply it to the HW. When the applied configuration is set as storable, it also saves it so it can be reapplied later on. 

_Apply(ListOfHandles)_
This API will get a list of handles and will apply all of them to the HW.
**This is the preferred method to apply multiple handles to the HW** since the service will optimize the data in all handles and will reduce the TT

### ReApplyStoredData APIs
_**This API's should be called only if the configuration is storable and it was previously applied and stored. Otherwise, it will throw exception**_
_**Currently not available for fuseConfig**_

_ReApplyStoredData(ListOfHandles)_
Reapply the stored data to the list of PatConfigHandles.

### GetStoredConfigurationNames APIs
_**Currently not available for fuseConfig**_
_GetStoredConfigurationNames()_
Gets the list of names of all stored configurations.

### IsSetPointHandleExist API
_IsSetPointHandleExist(module_name, group_name, setpoint)_
This API return SetPointHandleCheckerSymbol enum that contains, MODULE_DOESNT_EXIST, GROUP_DOESNT_EXIST, SETPOINT_DOESNT_EXIST, and EXIST.
It will first check for module -> group -> setpoint, and will return EXIST when all exist.


##Fuse Config Code Examples
###Before we continue to examples
All examples will refer to :
Register: CPU0
Label:  START_FUSE_WRITE
Pin:  P001
Pattern: P1 unless other was told

|Group Name | Fuse Name | Start Address Offset | End Address Offset|
|-----------|-----------|----------------------|-------------------|
|GROUP_A    | FUSE1     | 3                    | 0                 |
|GROUP_A    | FUSE2     | 5                    | 4                 |
|GROUP_B    | FUSE3     | 9                    | 6                 |
|FUSE4      | FUSE4     | 13                   | 10                |
|GROUP_B    | FUSE3_Valid_Bit| 14              | 14                |
|FUSE5      | FUSE5     | 16                   | 15                |
|FUSE6      | FUSE6     | 18                   | 17                |

![image.png](./.attachments/image-574338f1-df00-41e6-9e33-d701a3cffc05.png)

1. ### Fuse Config on one configuration with one fuse group, and the group contains only one fuse

Register: CPU0
Pattern: p1
Group : FUSE4, DATA : 0101
|Group Name | Fuse Name | Start Address Offset | End Address Offset|
|-----------|-----------|----------------------|-------------------|
|**FUSE4**      | FUSE4     | 13                   | 10                |

![image.png](./.attachments/image-eba99ab3-6fb0-4663-b3b5-61e54b43f37e.png)
FuseConfig.json
```json

{
  "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "MYFUSECONFIG",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Ratio": 1,
          "PatternsRegex": [
            "P1"
          ],
          "Registers": [
            "CPU0"
          ],
          "FuseGroup": [
            "FUSE4"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
]
```

Code Example:
```CSHARP
public class PatModifyTestMethod : TestMethodBase
{
    public override bool Verify()
    {
        this.MyFuseConfig = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MYFUSECONFIG");
        this.MyFuseConfig.SetData("1010");
        return true;
    }

    public override int Execute()
    {
        Prime.PatConfigService.Apply(this.MyFuseConfig);
        return true;
    }
}

```

```CSHARP
public class PatModifyTestMethod : TestMethodBase
{
    public override bool Verify()
    {
        this.MyFuseConfig1 = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MYFUSECONFIG1");
        this.MyFuseConfig1.SetData("00"); // NOTICE THE ASSIGNED VALUE
        this.MyFuseConfig2 = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MYFUSECONFIG2");
        this.MyFuseConfig2.SetData("10"); // NOTICE THE ASSIGNED VALUE

        this.AllFuseConfig.Add(MyFuseConfig1);
        this.AllFuseConfig.Add(MyFuseConfig2);

        return true;
    }

    public override int Execute()
    {
        Prime.PatConfigService.Apply(this.MyFuseConfig);
        return true;
    }
}

```

```json

    {
```

```json
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
          "Type": "PINDATA",
          "IsChannelLink": false,
          "Pin": "P001",
          "StartAddress": "StartLabel",
          "StartAddressOffset": 0,
          "EndAddress": "EndLabel",
          "EndAddressOffset": 0,
          "PatternsRegEx": [
            "P1*"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```


Code Example:
```CSHARP
public class PatModifyTestMethod : TestMethodBase
{
    public override bool Verify()
    {
        this.MyPatMod = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MyPatMod");
        this.MyPatMod.FillData("X+");
        return true;
    }

    public override int Execute()
    {
        Prime.PatConfigService.Apply(this.MyPatMod);
        return true;
    }
}
```

```CSHARP
public class PatModifyTestMethod : TestMethodBase
{
```

```CSHARP
    {
        this.MyPatMod1 = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MyPatMod1");
        this.MyPatMod.SetData("101");
        this.MyPatMod2 = Prime.PatConfigService.GetPatHandle("MyPatMod2");
        this.MyPatMod.SetData("1001");

        this.AllPatMod..Add( this.MyPatMod1);
        this.AllPatMod..Add( this.MyPatMod2);
        return true;
    }

    public override int Execute()
    {
        Prime.PatConfigService.Apply(this.AllPatMod1);

        return true;
    }
}
```
