#SetPoints
The setpoint feature allows the user to pre-define values to a group of configurations. The user can use the pre-defined values inside the code or by using common parameters

In the following examples, we will use the following configuration file.

All setpoint files need to end with ".PatConfigSetpoints.json". These files will be consumed by Aleph, see [ALEPH Files](https://dev.azure.com/mit-us/PRIME/_wiki/wikis/PRIME.wiki/181/Special-ENV-Variables-in-Prime?anchor=aleph_files)  for more setup information.

##Configuration Example for set points
```json

{
    "Configurations": [
    {
      "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
      "ConfigurationElement": [
        {
                    "Name": "CLK_A",
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "100",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "103",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "patConfig_setpoints.*"
                    ]
                },
{
                    "Name": "CLK_B",
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "104",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "107",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "patConfig_setpoints.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "waitStage",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "INSTRUCTION",
                    "Domain": "LEG",
                    "StartAddress": "8740",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "8740",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "patConfig_setpoints.*"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Name": "ONOff",
            "ConfigurationElement": [
                {
                    "Type": "PINDATA",
                    "IsChannelLink": false,
                    "Pin": "TDI",
                    "StartAddress": "200",
                    "StartAddressOffset": 0,
                    "EndAddress": "207",
                    "EndAddressOffset": 0,
                    "PatternsRegEx": [
                        "patConfig_setpoints"
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```
##Setpoint Json file
```json

{
  "Module": "Func",
  "Groups": [
    {
      "Name": "RatioChange",
      "Default": "0.5GHz",
      "SetPoints": [
        {
          "Name": "0.4GHz",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_A",
              "Ratio": 1,
              "PatternsRegEx": "fivr.*",
              "Data": "0100"
            },
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_B",
              "Ratio": 1,
              "PatternsRegEx": "fivr.*",
              "Data": "0010"
            },
            {
              "Name": "waitStage",
              "Ratio": 1,
              "PatternsRegEx": "fivr.*",
              "Data": "RPT 4"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Name": "0.5GHz",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_A",
              "Data": "0101"
            },
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_B",
              "Data": "1010"
            },
            {
              "Name": "waitStage",
              "Data": "RPT 5"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Name": "0.6GHz",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_A",
              "Data": "0110"
            },
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "ElementName": "CLK_B",
              "Data": "0110"
            },
            {
              "Name": "waitStage",
              "Data": "RPT 6"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "Name": "DynamicData",
      "Default": "MyDFF",
      "SetPoints": [
        {
          "Name": "RAW",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "Data": "01000000"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Name": "MyDFF",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "DFF": "WFR.SORT.SortRatio"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Name": "MyUserVar",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "UserVar": "MyCollection.MyUserVarRatio"
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "Name": "MySharedStorage",
          "Configurations": [
            {
              "Name": "boot_core_ratio",
              "SharedStorage": "DUT.MyRatio"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

```

### Module

A module represents a collection of groups
- A file has holds data for one module only
- There can be many files for the same module but the group name should be unique for the same module.
- different modules can have the same group names
- A module hold list of groups

### Group

A group define a collection of setpoints and a default setpoint
- Name: Name of the group, this will be used to get a handle for this group
- SetPoints: A list of set points
- Default: name of a default set point, this value must be defined in the setpoint list
- AllowNonMatchingSetPoints : An optional param, if true will allow that the configurations in set points will be diffrent (default false)

### SetPoints

This is a list that holds a collection of configuration names and values to apply.
By default all configurations need to be in all set points and it is being validated parsing the file (in init). In case we have a flag in the group with the value "AllowNonMatchingSetPoints" : true, this valdidation will be skipped. **Use this only if there is a real need that each setpoint will contain diffrent configurations.**
- Name: Name of the setpoint
- Configurations: a collection of configuration names and values to apply them


### Configuration

- Name: Name of the configuration to apply, this configuration must be pre-define in the PatMod Configuration file or FuseConfig file.
- ElementName: Optional field allow to define access to a specific element in the configuration
- Ratio: This field is not allowed by default, but it is optional if the fuse configuration mode is set to `FLAME`. This field is the way the setpoint has to provide ratio to apply the set point's data, and it's mandatory if the configuration is a fuse configuration.
- PatternsRegex: This field is not allowed by default, but it is optional if the fuse configuration mode is set to `FLAME`. This field is the way the setpoint has to provide the pattern's regular expression to filter the patterns used for the set point, and it's mandatory if the configuration is a fuse configuration.
- Data/DFF/UserVar/SharedStorage: Data source type to be applied, Only one of the types must be defined

| **Source**             | **Example** |
| --------------         | ----------- |
| Data                   | 100001 <br> 1+ <br> H+ |
| SharedStorage          | DUT.MyString1 <br> IP.MyString2 <br> LOT.MyString3 |
| UserVar                | MyCollection.UserVarName |
| DFF                    | DieId.OPType.DFFName.<Field Name (Optional)> <br>  CURRENT.CURRENT.MyDFF<br>U4.CURRENT.MyDFF.MyField<br>U4.PBIC.MyDFF<br><br> Use CURRENT keyword for DieID or Optype If the user want to current socket value and not set explicit|


## SetPoints API

### Get setpoint handle

After a file is defined and loaded, in order to use setpoint the user can get the handle for the set point

- GetSetPointHandle(module, group): this API will get a handle for a specific module and group
- GetSetPointHandle(module, group, plist): this API will get a handle for a specific module and group, the data will be applied only on patterns that relevant for the applied plist 

**Examples:**
let assume that we have 3 patterns :
   - patConfig_setpoints1
   - patConfig_setpoints2
   - patConfig_setpoints3

boot_core_ratio and waitStage configration have regular expression of patConfig_setpoints.*

And let assume that we have Plist1 that contain only patConfig_setpoints1

Plist1
- patConfig_setpoints2
- patConfig_setpoints11

**_Using API GetSetPointHandle(module, group)_**
GetSetPointHandle(Func, RatioChange)

The data will be applied to all 3 patterns.

**_Using API GetSetPointHandle(module, group, plist)_**
GetSetPointHandle(Func, RatioChange,Plist1)

The data will be applied on patterns in Plist1 that match the regular expression patConfig_setpoints.* that defined in the configurations, in our example, it will be applied to patConfig_setpoints2 and patConfig_setpoints11.

**_Using API GetSetPointHandleWithRegEx(module, group, regEx)_**
GetSetPointHandleWithRegEx(Func,RatioChange,.*1.*)

This Api will filter all pattern that match the configuration regEx ( in the example above all pattern that match patConfig_setpoints.*  are patConfig_setpoints1, patConfig_setpoints2  and patConfig_setpoints3) by the API regEx param "*1.*", this will return us patConfig_setpoints1 and patConfig_setpoints11


GetSetPointHandleWithRegEx(module, group, Plist1, regEx)
GetSetPointHandleWithRegEx(Func,RatioChange, Plist1, .*1.*)
This Api will filter all pattern that match the configuration regEx ( in the example above all pattern that match patConfig_setpoints.*  are patConfig_setpoints1, patConfig_setpoints2  and patConfig_setpoints3) by the API regEx param "*1.*", and also exist in the plist (Plist1) , the only pattern that match this is patConfig_setpoints11

###Apply setpoint 

  - ApplySetPoint(setPointName) - Apply all modification for the given set point name, for example :

   ApplySetPoint(0.4GHz) - will apply to "boot_core_ratio" configuration value "0100" and for "waitStage" configuration the value "RPT 4"

-   ApplySetPointDefault() - Apply default value for example in our example the default value in 0.5GHz, so the modification of set point 0.5GHz will be applied. 
To "boot_core_ratio" configuration value "0101" and for "waitStage" configuration the value "RPT 5"
## Using Common param
for detail information about common param read [Common Parameters](/[Internal%2DOnly]-Prime-Developers-Wiki/Wiki-master/General/Common-Parameters)

Using common param add the abilitin for the user to run set points before the instance execute or after the instance execute.
By adding the PatConfigSetPointsCommonParam.xml the user will get the following parameters : 

- SetPointsPreInstance : A list of setpoints configurations (separated by comma delimiter) module, group, and value to apply before instance execute, if the value isn't supplied default value will be used. the format is
<Module>:<Group>:<Setpoint value>,<Module>:<Group>
- SetPointsPostInstance : A list of setpoints configurations (separated by comma delimiter) module, group, and value to apply after instance execute, if the value isn't supplied default value will be used. the format is 
<Module>:<Group>:<Setpoint value>,<Module>:<Group>
- SetPointsPlistMode : This parameter as two values to choose from Local(default) and Global
  - Global : In this mode, we will ignore plist and apply to all patterns defined in the configuration file.
  - Local : In this mode we will look if we have value at "SetPointsPlistParamName" we will use this param value (see below) if the field is empty will try to find if we have param "Patlist" and get the plist name from there and apply the modification on all patterns in this plist that match the confiuration regex. if PatList will not be found the instance will fail verify.
- SetPointsPlistParamName : Relevand only when "setPointsPlistMode: is Local, this field hold the name of param at the current instance that holds the name of the plist. 
- SetPointsRegEx : regular expression to reduce the number of patterns in the configuration that match this regular expression, this regex will be applied to all set points

_examples:_


![image.png](./.attachments/image-18967029-54dd-43a2-afc4-76713254191c.png)



In this example we can see that setpoint 0.4GHhz for group RatioChange in module Func will be applied before instance execution and the default value will be applied for group setPointChange in module cache, also after instance execution default value will be applied for group RatioChange in module Func and OFF value will be applied to group setPointChange in module Cache.

![image.png](./.attachments/image-4a208d74-f083-45f7-8033-ee446c453608.png)
In this example the SetpointsPlistParamName contain the value Patlist, we can see that Patlist param hold a plist name that called MyPlist, the setpoints will be applied only to the patterns that match in plist MyPlist (see above Using API GetSetPointHandle(module, group, plist) )

![image.png](./.attachments/image-a60c95ad-5260-4077-a2e6-c8349775cb1c.png)
In this example the SetpointsPlistParamName is empty. The code will go to the Patlist param and will get the plist name the setpoints will be applied only to the patterns that match in plist MyPlist (see above Using API GetSetPointHandle(module, group, plist). If we don't habe Patlist param the instance will fail verify.
